$comment: Fallback/seed for the changelog page, RSS feed, and download button. In production the site reads the live feed (mac/release.command publishes releases.json to GitHub Pages on every ship); this file is used when that feed is unreachable or empty. Download links are derived from the GitHub release assets automatically — no URL needed. Entries with "notarized": false stay hidden until notarization completes.

changes.text: Design mode now clears stuck reveal blur so selected pages stay sharp.
Expired Claude sign-ins now show the real authentication error and the correct claude auth login action.
Authentication failures no longer appear as completed agent progress.
Failed focused edits keep the exact selected target and protect the last working source.
Design Copilot keeps the selected target, direct controls, quick actions, and Lunar Agent together in one focused dock.
Review mode now has a clear comment workflow with numbered pins, a live context bar, Fix All, and Done.
Design selections now use clearer element and shared-component states, a brief canvas tether, and calmer one-time motion.
Agent progress, questions, and finished changes stay visible in Design mode with immediate Keep or Undo actions.
The entire editor has a more polished, consistent light and dark appearance with stronger contrast and responsive controls.
Startup recovery no longer presents project trust UI during SwiftUI state initialization.
In-app Feedback, Roadmap, What’s New, and privacy-safe bug reporting powered by UserJot
Release notes now sync automatically to UserJot and the Lunarbeam website changelog
Redesigned home composer with the full agent, model, and reasoning controls before the first prompt
Component cards now include true Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile previews plus instant page-usage navigation
Updated Lunarbeam app icon across the app, Dock, installer, and release build
The Lunar Agent sidebar now uses a cleaner, flatter Codex-inspired design
Best-quality focused edits run thoroughly and continue automatically when they need more time
Component and element edits stay locked to the exact target selected by the user
New website generation now verifies that the agent changed real files and automatically continues incomplete builds
Model selection is passed correctly to every provider, with clearer handling for incompatible Codex versions
Interrupted generations save their recovery state and resume automatically after temporary connection failures
Component extraction locks conflicting canvas actions until the operation finishes
Renamed components keep their current identity for later edits, and shared previews no longer display the wrong page or component
Component previews are faster and sharper, preserve responsive layouts, and restore the correct Desktop height after switching breakpoints
Pexels stock photo picker in the image panel — search and apply free photos with one tap
Sitemap orphan warnings with one-click Fix with Site Doctor
Image overlay no longer glitches the canvas while adjusting tint
Overlay settings read back correctly after reselecting an image
Section reorder and Remove revert the preview when they cannot be saved
False orphan warnings while the sitemap is still mapping page links
Canvas snapshots prioritize visible pages; breakpoint mirrors stay current after edits

releases.title: Ship straight to your own Vercel

changes.text: One-click publish to your own Vercel account. Connect once, then deploy to production from inside the app.
Attach a custom domain with copy-paste DNS records and verification that re-checks itself, live.
A make-public toggle drops the sign-in wall so anyone can view the site.
The publish console now bundles Deploy, Domain and Settings into one place.

releases.title: Components, build once and place anywhere

changes.text: Promote any section into a reusable component in a single step.
Drop components between sections from a palette. You never touch code.
Shared header and footer are protected by default, so a page tweak can never rewrite them.
Import your own HTML onto the canvas and pull it apart into components.

releases.title: A live map of your whole site

changes.text: The Sitemap inspector maps every incoming and outgoing link, plus each page level.
Orphan-page warnings the moment a page loses its last link.
Rename a route and every link across the header and footer updates automatically.
Dynamic-route templates render data-driven pages from one design.
Smart section detection. Just say “redesign the hero” and it scopes the work itself.
Edits apply as precise diffs. Everything around them stays byte-for-byte identical.
Drag to reorder sections right on the canvas, saved back to the source.
Self-healing builds now recover cleanly from a wider range of errors.
Prompt a website and watch it build live, section by section.
Screens for fast HTML pages, and Pro Build for real Next.js projects.
An infinite 60 fps canvas with View, Design, Sitemap and Components modes.
Runs on your existing Claude plan. No API key, no extra bill.
